reviews.review_description:
I just tried Cave Grave on Quest 3, and I absolutely loved it! Visually, the game is outstanding: the realism of the textures, the flashlight lighting effects, and the details on the rock faces are top-notch. You feel completely there.
However, the atmosphere is so gripping and realistically rendered that it comes with a warning: this is a game only non-claustrophobic people should try! The tightness of the cramped spaces and the sheer verticality of the pits make the sense of confinement incredibly real.
If you love pure exploration and intense VR sensations, go for it—this game is a gem!

Hi Cave Grave team,
First of all, congrats on the amazing work on the game! The immersion and caving experience on the Meta Quest 3 are incredible, and it’s a blast to explore your environments.
As a big fan of both VR and exploration, I had an idea I wanted to share. Are you planning future updates with new caves inspired by the real world?
If so, there is an absolute must-have location that would be a legendary addition to the game: the Hang Sơn Đoòng cave in Vietnam (the largest natural cave in the world).
With its massive dimensions (passages over 200 meters high), underground river, giant skylights, and its very own jungle, this cave has unique visual and exploration potential. Being able to experience and visualize it at a 1:1 scale through Cave Grave’s movement mechanics and the power of the Quest 3 would be an absolute dream for the community.
An environment this grandiose would push the game's sense of scale and verticality to a whole new level.
Thanks again for making such a cool game, and thank you for taking the time to read feedback from your players! Can't wait to see what you guys have in store for the future.
Best of luck with the development,

reviews.review_title: Helped some with my fear of mortality
reviews.review_description:
This sounds weird, I know. But as someone who had learned of the story of John Jones and the nutty putty cave years before this game came out, this was an experience. When I had first heard John’s story, it sent me into a multi-month fight or flight stage panic attack. And even till this day I wrestled with my fears revolving mortality. When I heard that Cave Crave included the nutty putty cave in an update, those fears started to resurface and I told myself I wasn’t going to touch it.
Fast forward a few months, and I decided that this would possibly be the safest way to confront those fears. So I did it. And I by no means am saying this game cured me, but by entering the cave I had no idea I was about to embark on one of the most impactful visualizations of spiritual rebirth that has ever been captured in human recreation.
I guess they really do call it “the birth canal” for a reason. 10/10

This game not only looks good but feels good. I see lots of people that can't even run the game which is sad but it's usually because they have in old headsets with not a lot of storage. I can't wait until the next update. I hope you can play multi-player in the horror mode. The story line is also good so far.

I love the game but one thing that confuses me….i had to delete bonelab,down shot and others games just for the spray paint update….i did it and for some reason i was able….TO GET BONELAB,DOWNSHOT,AND OThERS GAMES WITHOUT ANY PROBLEMS….but I love the game and it fun as heck but sometimes people draw dihhs in painting mode and also cuss or write cuz cuss words but I recommend yall bye this game because my whole live time playing this game I only encountered like about 5 people who cuss’s and 5 people who draws weird stuff so it’s pretty protective and I would rate it a 6-10 stars but since those others 5,s don’t exist I have to rate it a 5

I’ve been playing this game for a while now I even bought the other games by the way she’s doing the Discord if you’re reading this either way it’s worth your bye. The only thing that I can suggest that they should add is yes more features such as the multiplayer personality and stuff like that, you know your character, but the main thing needed ad is more side objectives things like you can go on missions to collect money or something have collectibles instead of just redoing story over and over again the game has a little to it but it is insanely high-quality ￼￼

I’ve looked into spelunking before but that’s not a sport or hobby I was Willy to pursue. I saw some videos in cave crave and I figured I’d see what it would be like to go spelunking without having to leave home. 

The graphics, detail of the caves, lighting and tools are some of the best I’ve seen so far. The story mode was meh but there’s only so much to work with when you’re talking about people who explore caves for fun or for work. 

It does get a little deep with the true life nutty putty cave and the story of John Jones, seeing how tight of a space he crawled into and knowing that I would have never gone through the birth canal or the aorta myself is a neat way to explore the subject. 

Only 4/5 because of the story and the few caves to explore but worth the price of admission.

It was really fun exploring nutty putty, and all the levels, even horror levels. I kept dying alot in the "eight legs" level. But after i magically completed it (finally), i just HAD to bully that one spider that kept killing me 😂. This game made me overcome my claustrophobia. The game is really Epic!!

Also worth every dollar and cent i needed for the game!

Message to developers: dont give up!

I tried the game I was so excited and The second I joined I couldn't even crawl through a hole and literally just makes me suffocate but I literally see other people crawling through the same hole perfectly fine 😭

developer_response.body:
If you can't go through narrowings on your own just open settings in your game tablet and set auto exhale feature on - you'll be passing tight squeezes automatically. Cheers!

Piotr Surmacz, 3R Games CEO

Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_title: Great game but.. devs please read
reviews.review_description:
Great job devs; the game design is quite good and immersive but please…

Spoiler Alert:

The horror factor is the biggest potential of this game, yet the floating rocks and the white and red probes hovering around are not immersive or horrifying or even stressful at all! You can add some fictional elements but please keep the game’s realistic look.

I originally bought the game for the horror aspect which I thought would be something similar to the movie “the descent 1/2” (please watch them and see how cave horror is done) and what about some extra tarantulas or snakes crawling around?

Don’t get me wrong the game’s realistic and so good, but the horror, if done right and added more maps, would really take the game to a whole other level.

Please add glowing sticks as well.

reviews.review_title: Very fun but needs more side objectives
reviews.review_description: I have a few ideas, like a side objective for the abyss where you travel to grab the object, and when you come back you can actually find a secret path down where you have to cross the abyss on the bottom to grab new skins. Or have skins for items in secrets. The game needs more secrets and that would make it a lot of fun.

This game is great its the best 14 bucks i ever spend on a virtual item the graphics are great but i would enjoy a sandbox Mode like the nutty putty cave but with more caves or incidents but regardless to that its a great game 🤙

I was on the John Jones Nutty Putty Cave Rescue Team back in 2009. It's been 16 years since I've been in the cave and going through this tour helped me remember some of the passages I've forgotten. It was fun taking a walk down memory lane and I think it will help those who weren't involved get a sense of what we were up against. Well done. The rest of the game was fun too. It's nice to push through ridiculously tight passages without the physical drain that comes with it.

When I first saw this game it just looked like a lot of boring repetitive crawling through narrow cramped lava tubes over and over. This team has done an amazing job of continually updating it more and more since launch by  adding real world locations to add a lot more variety to explore, and now even underwater and spelunking happening - I can't wait to see the new announced content launching in July!! I'd like to see rescue missions added as future DLC if that's possible, along with getting to explore the Chauvet Cave shown in The Cave of Forgotten Dreams documentary if there's ever any possibility of that. This game has gone rapidly from mediocre to amazing and I hope to see tons of more real-world locations getting added in the future.

The story very interesting which I feel not enough, people talking about, ￼and the game is very realistic you have ￼to clean your glove, so you don’t sleep while climbing Your flashlight has a battery and you have to place down, markers, ￼so you know where you have been before the￼ nuttyputty cave, ￼is really cool, and they’re adding new stuff soon I think it’s gonna take a bit more to be a 5 star game, but for now 4 stars ￼

this game is genuinely just beautiful it's slow passed and a lot of fun one of the modes has you explore a real life accurate cave and it gives history on the cave without feeling overwhelming the narrator has been in this cave at least 20 times me and my friend got this together and we are having a lot of fun exploring the different caves and it's super calming with the music and the narration i recommend this to anyone who plays vr the prices super good for the game

Being able to go through the nutty putty cave was so awesome and gave me a sense of exploration and wonder that the story caves couldn't quite match. It would be amazing if we could visit more real caves! Even if it was a dlc or something, I would still totally go for it!

this game is an absolute masterpiece and makes me feel like im in the cave. i love the feeling of exploration and seeing the cool parts of the cave. I love the nutty putty cave and how i get to see the history of the cave as i go down which is a fun aspect and i love that i can see just where john jones got stuck. RIP. 

to the cave crave team,
i feel like adding some more real life caves or just more caves in general, which id probably what you guys are working on, would make it a lot more fun because of the lack of content in my opinion. My local cave hell hole in santa cruz, or IXL cave which is its official name could be very cool to add as at the bottom there is faces in the wall. it is a very small cave meaning it would take less effort than some but it has a lot of cool factor. i feel like adding some more features like the use of ropes and rappeling in the future could be cool and i also feel like it would be really fun to add watwr sections in a lot of caves, as many have aquifers and waterfalls. this could also make rock slippery. one issue i have noticed however is that sometimes your hands glitch and one or two disapear which made me have to delete my progress in one of the story caves because i could not get to the top.

thank you very much for this amazing game and i cant wait to see what comes next.
Multiplayer is amaizing

reviews.review_title: This is an absolutely amazing game!
reviews.review_description: People do say this game is very hard, but if you practice and keep trying it can be an amazing experience. The new Nutty Putty update is amazing I really enjoyed exploring this forgotten cave. (I’ve actually been in it before) The cave is extremely accurate to the real thing and brought back lots of memories. The horror mode is very exhilarating and scary. But it was obviously well made because. Well… it really is scary! (I only recommend this mode to people of 13 plus)The story mode of this game is great. I love the voice lines and how deep this story goes. (No spoilers!) Every one of these levels are different and difficult. This game allows you to test your skills in many different ways. All of the mechanics work pretty much perfectly after updates and fixes. People who did try the game and quit because it was “too hard.” Please try the game again I’m sure you will enjoy it. It truly has changed a lot. And people who haven’t started yet. Get ready to be addicted!
Love it scary but FUN

The fact that multiplayer is coming soon too? This game is shaping up to be something awesome.
